channel.title: Global Sanctions News (Custom RSS)
channel.description: Auto-generated feed from rendered Global Sanctions news list

item.title: EU updates South Sudan minister’s travel ban
item.description: The EU has updated its statement of reasons for listing Michael Makuei Leuth under Regulation (EU) 2015/735, the EU’s South Sudan sanctions list. Mr Leuth was South Sudan’s Minister for Information an

item.title: UK launches parliamentary inquiry into deterring Russian aggression – evidence by 8 July
item.description: The UK has launched a parliamentary inquiry on the UK’s approach to deterring Russian aggression, with a focus on imposing costs and constraints – press release. Parliamentary inquiries are convene

item.title: Licence Enhancement Act passes House Foreign Affairs Committee
item.description: The Bureau of Industry and Security License Administration Enhancement Act has passed the House Foreign Affairs Committee and has been introduced to the US Senate – press release. It was introduced on

item.title: Russia amends law countering ‘unfriendly actions’ by the US and foreign states
item.description: Russia has amended its Federal Law on measures of influence (counteraction) against unfriendly actions of the United States of America and other foreign states. The amendments are now all in force and

item.title: China adds 10 US companies to export control list & places procurement ban on 46 companies
item.description: China has added 10 US companies to its list of entities subject to export controls and has separately banned Chinese buyers from procuring products manufactured by 46 US companies. See our China expor

item.title: US adds ISIS facilitators to counterterrorism sanctions list
item.description: The US has added 3 people and 6 entities to its EO 13224 counterterrorism sanctions list for facilitating financial transactions on behalf of ISIS – press release. Miloud Abderrahmane, a French n

item.title: EU agrees to renew Russia sectoral sanction list for 12 months
item.description: The EU has agreed to renew its Regulation (EU) 833/2014 sectoral sanctions list for 12 months. Ordinarily, this list is renewed for 6 months. The EU has also confirmed that it is working on its 21st s

item.title: Belgium submits draft law on EU sanctions criminalisation directive to Parliament
item.description: Belgium has submitted its draft law transposing the EU’s sanctions criminalisation directive (Directive (EU) 2024/1226) for consideration by Parliament. The Directive seeks to harmonise penalties for

item.title: Norway releases 2025 defence export control statistics
item.description: Norway has published its report on exports of defence equipment in Norway in 2025 – summary. The report sets out the regulatory changes in 2025, for defence and dual-use trade. In August 2025, th

item.title: Russian Supreme Court issues overview of sanctions disputes
item.description: Russia’s Supreme Court has published a thematic review on how commercial (arbitrazh) courts apply Russian countersanctions legislation and its laws on unfriendly states. “Unfriendly states” are countr
